history: The client has a history of relationships that sometimes shift into romantic connections and past conflicts in relationships following expressions of anger. They have a complex relationship with their parents involving obligations, and they live with a supportive nephew who helps with bills. The client experiences persistent auditory hallucinations linked to a female figure from childhood, contributing to anxiety and fear.
intermediate_belief: If I make a mistake, it will prove that I am not good enough and I will lose control over my life.
intermediate_belief_depression: If I can't control these voices, I will never be able to function properly and my life will always be a struggle.
coping_strategies: The client engages in procrastination and avoidance to manage the fear of making mistakes. They rely on medication to manage symptoms, though it provides only partial relief.
situation: Difficulty completing work tasks due to fear of making mistakes.
auto_thought: I can't do this right; I'm going to mess everything up.
behavior: The client procrastinates and avoids tasks, feeling overwhelmed by the voices telling them what to do.
